verdicts.reasoning: On page 605 verse 15 ends cleanly with a middle point after ἐστιν, and verse 16 begins separately below with ἀσεβεῖς δὲ; this matches A and C for v.15, while B has accidentally run v.16 into v.15.

verdicts.verdict_greek: ἀσεβεῖς δὲ ταῖς χερσὶ καὶ τοῖς λόγοις προσεκαλέσαντο αὐτόν, φίλον ἡγησάμενοι αὐτὸν ἐτάκησαν, καὶ συνθήκην ἔθεντο πρὸς αὐτόν, ὅτι ἄξιοί εἰσιν τῆς ἐκείνου μερίδος εἶναι.
verdicts.reasoning: The scan on page 605 clearly prints ταῖς χερσὶ (not χερσὶν) and has punctuation exactly as in A; Cambridge LXX and Tischendorf likewise support the Swete/Vaticanus wording here against the differing Rahlfs punctuation-only presentation.

notes: Adjudicated directly from Swete page 605 (image labeled 622 in the attachment set); other attached pages are unrelated to Wisdom 1:15-16.
